summary: Iteration 15 Lighthouse hardening fully verified. Backend: 5/5 new iter15 tests pass — all 7 security headers present on /api/ (X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy locked-down, COOP same-origin, HSTS max-age=31536000, CSP frame-ancestors 'none'); same headers carried through the public brief PDF route; PDF still 100KB+ with %PDF- magic; admin cookie login → auth/verify → pilot-requests round-trip works; TEST_-prefixed pilot intake returns test_bypass status. Frontend: all 21 React.lazy routes return 200, render real content, Suspense fallback (data-testid='route-suspense-fallback') resolves cleanly on every deep-link, zero console errors, zero CSP violations on initial routing. Admin login E2E works (cookie httpOnly, 91 leads visible, stats render). PostHog initialises after consent accept (7 network requests to us.i.posthog.com / us-assets.i.posthog.com, window.posthog.__loaded === true). ONE CSP regression found: PostHog session-recorder creates a Web Worker from a blob: URL which the new script-src directive does not allow.

integration_issues.issue: CSP violation: 'Creating a worker from blob:... violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' https://us.i.posthog.com https://us-assets.i.posthog.com https://assets.emergent.sh https://static.cloudflareinsights.com".' Page-view capture & autocapture still work (7 PostHog requests succeed), but session-replay/recorder cannot spawn its Worker. Fix: add `worker-src 'self' blob:` (or include `blob:` in script-src) to the CSP meta tag in /app/frontend/public/index.html line 23.

action_items: Add `worker-src 'self' blob:` directive (or extend script-src with `blob:`) to the CSP meta in /app/frontend/public/index.html so PostHog session-recorder Worker can boot. Without this, session-replay silently fails after consent accept; page-view + autocapture telemetry still flows.

Optional belt-and-braces: also add `worker-src 'self' blob:` to the backend SecurityHeadersMiddleware CSP in /app/backend/server.py line 263 so that any HTML served by the API (none today, but defensive) stays consistent.
critical_code_review_comments: SecurityHeadersMiddleware uses headers.setdefault() — correct choice: lets per-route headers (e.g. PDF Cache-Control) take precedence. Header set is minimal and correct.
frame-ancestors 'none' on the API CSP is the right scope — API returns JSON/PDF only, so omitting script-src/style-src directives keeps the policy tight without noise.

App.js lazy boundary structure is correct: LandingPage remains eager (LCP target), every other route is lazy, single Suspense above <Routes> with a branded fallback. Verified live: fallback resolves on every deep-link, never sticks.

CSP meta in public/index.html is missing `worker-src` — modern browsers fall back to script-src for workers, but blob: workers still need explicit allowance. PostHog session-recorder is the only consumer affected today; if PostHog session-replay is a product requirement, this is a HIGH priority fix, otherwise MINOR.
Cookie consent banner data-testids are complete and clean (cookie-consent-banner / -accept / -reject / -close / -policy-link).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (21/21 routes render clean; 1 CSP worker block on post-consent PostHog session recorder)

test_credentials: ADMIN_TOKEN=dev-admin-trs-2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md) — verified working via cookie flow.
seed_data_creation: One synthetic TEST_LIGHTHOUSE pilot-request was inserted by test_pilot_request_stub_for_test_prefix. The admin dashboard total jumped to 91 (+1 over iter14's 90-ish baseline). No real emails sent (test_bypass).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter15 (Lighthouse perf/security batch) is solid. ONE follow-up: CSP needs `worker-src 'self' blob:` for PostHog session recorder. Everything else is green — all 21 routes lazy-load cleanly, all 7 security headers wire through correctly, admin auth and PDF endpoints unaffected. Regression test file is /app/backend/tests/test_iter15_security_headers.py — re-run before any future CSP changes.
rca of the issue: Root cause: the CSP meta tag in /app/frontend/public/index.html (line 23) sets script-src to a fixed allowlist that excludes the `blob:` scheme. PostHog's posthog-recorder.js instantiates a Web Worker from a Blob (URL.createObjectURL(blob)) for its session-replay buffering. Modern browsers fall back to script-src when worker-src is absent, so the Worker constructor is blocked. Reproduction: load /, click cookie-consent-accept, watch DevTools → Console for 'Creating a worker from blob: ... violates ... script-src ...'. Mitigation: append `; worker-src 'self' blob:` to the CSP content attribute (or add `blob:` to script-src). One-line fix.
